text.title: Backup Strategies (3-2-1-1-0) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounded in 3-2-1-1-0 + Veeam / Microsoft / Google SRE incident learnings.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare ≥3 copies, ≥2 distinct media types, ≥1 offsite, ≥1 immutable. Restore-verification job MUST exist + report 0 errors over the last verification window.
rule.rationale: Traditional 3-2-1 fails against ransomware. The added immutable copy + restore-test close the two attack vectors (mass-encryption + silent-corruption).

rule.statement: Immutable storage MUST use COMPLIANCE mode (S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE / Azure Immutable Blob policy-locked). GOVERNANCE mode is forbidden for prod backups.
rule.rationale: GOVERNANCE allows users with the right IAM permission to delete; COMPLIANCE cannot be overridden even by the AWS root account.

rule.statement: An automated restore-verification job MUST run at least weekly (nightly for tier-1 data) and emit a pass/fail metric to monitoring.
rule.rationale: Backups never tested are not backups. The first time you discover the dump is corrupted MUST NOT be the day you need it.

rule.statement: Spec MUST name explicit RPO and RTO numeric targets. 'As fast as possible' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without numeric targets, the team cannot choose between cheap (S3 Glacier, RTO 12h) and fast (warm standby DB, RTO 5 min). Vague targets always over-engineer or under-engineer.

rule.statement: The offsite copy MUST live in a different failure domain (different region + different cloud account, or different cloud provider entirely). Same-region different-AZ is not offsite.
rule.rationale: Region-wide outages happen (us-east-1 multiple times). Same-account compromise (stolen IAM creds) takes both copies. Different failure domain is the only real protection.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
